compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Why
`test_completion_floor_e2e.py::test_non_regression_floor_is_review_only` (`:178-198`) asserts,
via `inspect.getsource` over the floor functions, that tokens like `sign_manifest`,
`transition`, `reopen(` are ABSENT from their source, and that `completion_floor` does not
appear in `rebar.llm.completion`. A pure change-detector: a rename / helper / indirect call
evades it, and it exercises no runtime behavior. VALIDATED.

Context on what is testable: the floor's DECISION logic (which findings drop / are preserved /
resurface on reopen) is ALREADY behaviorally covered by the sibling tests in this file
(`test_floor_drops_and_preserves_per_gold_labels`, `test_floor_inactive_is_byte_identical`,
`test_reopen_resurfaces_dropped_finding`). And `_apply_completion_floor_to_verdict` is a PURE
verdict→verdict transform (no `repo_root`, no store/LLM I/O), so "it doesn't sign/transition"
cannot be shown by a store snapshot or by spying a close-path function it structurally never
calls — those tests are vacuous. The genuine, non-vacuous, non-duplicative behavioral content
the old test was reaching for is the floor's **observable purity contract**: it only
re-partitions findings and never emits any signing/attestation side-output.

## What
Replace the source-token test with a behavioral **purity-contract** test that runs the real
floor over the existing gold fixture (a case that drops a finding) and asserts on OBSERVABLE
state:
1. **Findings conserved (no create/lose/rewrite):** the multiset of finding ids across
   `advisory ∪ dropped` after the floor equals the set before — the floor only moves findings
   between the two lists (this is the observable analogue of "never signs/transitions/reopens";
   a floor that fabricated or dropped-without-recording a finding would fail it).
2. **No signing/attestation side-output:** the returned/mutated verdict grows NO
   `signature` / `attestation` / `attestations` key, and no new attestation KIND — the floor's
   only additions are the `dropped` list and the `coverage` narrowing annotation. (Assert the
   set of top-level verdict keys added by the floor ⊆ {`dropped`, and coverage sub-keys}.)
3. **Inputs not mutated:** the `cmap` argument and each finding's own content dict are unchanged
   by identity/value — the floor re-partitions list membership only, it does not rewrite the
   caller's inputs. (Deep-copy the inputs before the call; assert equal after.)
4. Keep the genuine artifact-contract assertion `attest._MANIFEST_PREFIX == "plan-review"`
   (no NEW attestation kind exists) — a real invariant, not a source grep.

Delete the `inspect.getsource` token-absence assertions (for the floor functions and for
`rebar.llm.completion`). The confinement-of-the-floor-to-plan-review property is NOT asserted
behaviorally, because it is a negative over a pure, structurally-separated function (any such
test is vacuous against the current code); it is left to the module structure, and this ticket
converts the false-confidence source grep into the real, observable purity assertions above.

## Scope
- `tests/unit/test_completion_floor_e2e.py` — replace `test_non_regression_floor_is_review_only`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A behavioral test runs the floor over the gold fixture and asserts findings are CONSERVED
      (the id set across `advisory ∪ dropped` is identical before/after — nothing fabricated or
      silently lost).
- [ ] The test asserts the floor adds NO `signature`/`attestation`/`attestations` key to the
      verdict (its only additions are `dropped` + the coverage narrowing).
- [ ] The test asserts the floor does not mutate its `cmap` input or the finding content dicts
      (pure re-partition) — verified against deep copies taken before the call.
- [ ] No `inspect.getsource` token-absence assertion remains; the
      `attest._MANIFEST_PREFIX == "plan-review"` artifact contract is retained.
- [ ] `make test` green.

file_impact.reason: replace getsource token-absence test with spy-based side-effect test

compiled_state.title: Replace completion-floor review-only source-token test with a spy-based side-effect test
